CS & Math · Duke University

Developer. Founder. Researcher.
Building and shipping things that matter.

Hi, I'm Siddhant, a Computer Science and Mathematics student at Duke. I love building things at the cusp of research and real products - stuff that's interesting to make and actually matters once it's out. I'm grateful to say that some of what I've shipped is already making an impact. Outside of coding and research, you'll find me at the gym, playing cricket and FIFA. I'm always down to meet interesting people, so feel free to reach out!

Cool Stuff I've Built
back-d↗
In Progress
  • Full-stack NFL player web-based equity marketplace (back-d.com) where fans buy and sell athlete shares like stocks using an AMM pricing engine with a pre-defined platform fee and Stripe-powered deposits
  • Built with Next.js 14, Node.js, PostgreSQL, and Prisma ORM, featuring Clerk auth, live price charts, and fan portfolio dashboards
Next.jsNode.jsPostgreSQLStripe
Custom CUDA Kernel↗
High Performance
  • Built a custom CUDA transformer attention kernel from scratch, achieving 15.6x faster inference than baseline and 1.9x faster than PyTorch on Duke HPC (RTX 2080 Ti) via memory coalescing and shared memory tiling
  • Diagnosed less than 1% GPU utilization through CUDA event profiling and optimized memory access patterns, reducing latency from 1.88ms to 0.069ms at seq=512
CUDAC++PyTorchHPC
Verity↗
AI-Powered
  • AI-powered Chrome extension that scores every Gmail sender's trustworthiness from 0-100 in real time, detecting phishing attempts, spoofed identities, and urgency manipulation tactics used by bad actors
  • Built with Manifest V3 and Vanilla JS on the frontend, backed by a Python FastAPI server handling WHOIS domain lookups, rule-based NLP analysis, and sender reputation scoring
